task_completion.description: Verify that the agent successfully logged into the NOSH ChartingSystem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH dashboard or patient search screen is visible in the web browser, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Verify that the target patient's chart is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient's name or ID is visible in the active patient context, header, or chart summary.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigated to the problem list or issues section of the patient's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section titled 'Issues', 'Problem List', 'Diagnoses', or similar is visible and active in the EHR interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Essential Hypertension' or 'I10' is entered as the condition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form for adding or editing an issue shows 'Essential Hypertension' or 'I10' in the diagnosis/condition input field.

task_completion.description: Verify that the onset date is set to 2024-08-15.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date '2024-08-15' (or equivalent format like '08/15/2024') is entered in the onset date field.

task_completion.description: Verify that the status of the problem is set to Active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The status dropdown, toggle, or radio button is set to 'Active' for the new condition.

task_completion.description: Verify that the new problem list entry is saved and visible in the patient's active problem list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The problem list displays 'Essential Hypertension' or 'I10' with the correct onset date and active status in the saved list of issues, outside of edit mode.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the web interface to add the problem list entry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser with the NOSH interface being interacted with, not a terminal executing SQL commands or API requests.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the NOSH ChartingSystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches a typical EHR system (NOSH), not a generic text editor or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent didn't just type the expected data into a URL or search bar to make it appear on screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data is entered into proper form fields within the application, not just visible in the browser address bar or a generic search field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: NOSH ChartingSystem is an open-source electronic health record system. The target condition is 'Essential Hypertension' with ICD-10 code 'I10'. The onset date must be '2024-08-15' (or equivalent format like 08/15/2024). The status must be 'Active'. The problem list might be referred to as 'Issues' or 'Diagnoses' in the NOSH interface.
